entries.name: abs
entries.description: This function returns the absolute value of a specified number.

entries.name: ach
entries.description: This function returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine of a specified number.

entries.name: acos
entries.description: acos returns the arc cosine of a value. The result is expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

entries.name: ACTION
entries.description: Entry dispatch block frequently used to route object or screen actions to Gosub labels.
invariants: Return after the Case block.
Keep labels explicit and action names stable.
entries.example: $ACTION / Case ACTION / When "OUVRE" : Gosub OUVRE / Endcase / Return
notes: Strongly represented in snippets and corpus.

entries.name: Actzo
entries.description: Actzo activates all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously grayed.
entries.example: Actzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Additm
entries.description: Observed UI composition command that adds an item dynamically.
entries.signature: Additm POSITION Titled TEXT To TARGET
invariants: Keep the target explicit.

entries.name: addline
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Collection Add Line Event.

entries.name: Addmen
entries.description: Observed command that adds a menu entry dynamically.
invariants: Use stable identifiers.
Keep the action traceable.

entries.name: addmonth
entries.description: addmonth allows you to add a given number of months to a date.

entries.name: adelete
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: CRUD Delete Event.

entries.name: adelline
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Collection Delete Line Event.

entries.name: adxcio
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: adxdcs
entries.description: adxdcs is a pivot year used to determine the century when the year is two digits.
entries.example: adxdcs - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxdir
entries.description: This function returns the path of the directory where the $$PRODUCT execution engine run-time is installed on the current process server.
entries.example: adxdir - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxdlrec
entries.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last Delete instruction. It must be called after the Delete instruction.
entries.example: adxdlrec - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxfname
entries.description: adxfname returns the name of the columns of an opened table. It is an array of string variables present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the **_abv_** abbreviation.
entries.example: adxfname - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxftl
entries.description: adxftl is a variable used to set a read grouping factor in a number of records.
entries.example: adxftl - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxifs
entries.description: adxifs is a character string variable of length 1 that contains the field separator used when reading or writing sequential files by the instructions Rdseq and Wrseq.
entries.example: adxifs - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxioa
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: adxirs
entries.description: adxirs is a character string variable of length 2 maximum that contains the record separator used when reading or writing sequential files by the instructions Rdseq and Wrseq.
entries.example: adxirs - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxium
entries.description: Adxium is a numeric value that defines the coding method used for writing or reading character strings in a text file by the instructions  Wrseq and Rdseq.
entries.example: adxium - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxlog
entries.description: adxlog is a variable that states if a database transaction has already been started (by Trbegin).
entries.example: adxlog - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxmac
entries.description: adxmac is a function used to identify the server in which the current folder, or one of its reference folder, is installed. It also allows you to obtain the network name of the client (in Version 7 mode, the client is the node.js server).

entries.name: adxmda
entries.description: adxmda is a numeric value that determines the current angular mode. It is used for the functions that return or use angles.
entries.example: adxmda - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxmother
entries.description: Adxmother is a character string type variable array that contains the names of the reference folder of the current folder.
entries.example: adxmother - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxmso
entries.description: adxmso is an internal integer variable that indicates the maximum number of files that can be simultaneously opened by Openi, Openo, and Openio.
entries.example: adxmso - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxmto
entries.description: adxmto is an internal integer variable that indicates the maximum number of tables that can be simultaneously opened by File.
entries.example: adxmto - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxnfs
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: adxpam
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: adxpid
entries.description: adxpid returns the process **ID** that executes the $$PRODUCT code on the $$PRODUCT server.

entries.name: adxpno
entries.description: adxpno gives access to the stacked scripts called with Call, Fmet, or func.

entries.name: adxsca
entries.description: adxsca is an internal string variable that stores characters used by formatting functions.
entries.example: adxsca - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxseek
entries.description: adxseek returns the position of the read / write pointers on sequential files.

entries.name: adxsqlrec
entries.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last ExecSql instruction. It must be called after an ExecSql instruction that inserts, updates, or deletes records. This function returns 0 after ExecSql instructions that execute DDL statements like CREATE TABLE or DROP TABLE.
entries.example: adxsqlrec - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtcp
entries.description: adxtcp returns the IP service number used to connect on the $$PRODUCT process for the server. It returns a numerical value.

entries.name: adxtct
entries.description: adxtct is a Char variable that indicates the name of the table where the sequence numbers are stored.
entries.example: adxtct - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtlk
entries.description: adxtlk is a Char variable. It defines the name of the table that stores the logical locks.
entries.example: adxtlk - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtms
entries.description: adxtms is a Char variable that defines the name of the table where the messages are stored.
entries.example: adxtms - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtrans
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: adxtuc
entries.description: adxtuc is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 33. It allows you to define accepted characters in additional data types used for formatting data.
entries.example: adxtuc - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtul
entries.description: adxtul is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 1. It allows you to define data types used for formatting data.
entries.example: adxtul - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtut
entries.description: adxtut is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 17. It allows you to define sub-data types associated with additional data types used for formatting data.
entries.example: adxtut - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxtyp
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: adxuid
entries.description: adxuid returns a unique identifier for a connection.

entries.name: adxuprec
entries.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last Update instruction, and it must be called after the instruction.
entries.example: adxuprec - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: adxwrb
entries.description: adxwrb stores the number of buffered database write operations.
entries.example: adxwrb - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Affzo
entries.description: Affzo displays all or part of the fields in a mask.
entries.example: Affzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: AGPOINT
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Entry point .
entries.example: GPOINT = Value} : Gosub ENTREE From EXEFNC

entries.name: ainsert
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: CRUD Insert Event.

entries.name: AllocGroup
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: allocgrp
entries.description: This built-in property returns the allocation group of an instance. Allocgrp is essentially a technical property and is rarely used.
entries.example: allocgrp - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: anasql
entries.description: Anasql is used to parse a SQL sentence and return in an integer array the number and the types of values that are returned by the SQL sentence.
entries.example: anasql - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: and
entries.description: and performs a logical 'and' between two boolean values.
entries.example: and - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: anp
entries.description: anp calculates the number of permutations (for example, ordered arrangements) of **p** objects taken from **n**.

entries.name: Append
entries.description: Append allows to concatenate a char or CLOB value to a char or CLOB variable.

entries.name: aquery
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Query Event.

entries.name: ar2
entries.description: ar2 is a function that rounds a number to two digits. The method used is the method known as Round half away from zero

entries.name: Archive
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: aread
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: CRUD Read Event.

entries.name: arr
entries.description: ar2 is a function that rounds a number to a specified increment. The method used is the method known as <a target="_blank" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/rounding#Tie-breaking">half away from zero.

entries.name: As
entries.description: This keyword is used in several syntaxes with the following keywords:

entries.name: asc
entries.description: This keyword is used in the Sorta instruction to describe an ascending sorting order on all parameters. See the Sorta for more information.
entries.example: asc - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: ascii
entries.description: ascii returns the code of the first character of a string.

entries.name: ash
entries.description: This function returns the inverse hyperbolic sine of a specified number.

entries.name: asin
entries.description: asin returns the arc sine of a value. The result is expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

entries.name: assign
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Assign - assigns a value to a variable whose name is evaluated at runtime.

entries.name: atan
entries.description: atan returns the arc tangent of a value. The result is expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

entries.name: atan2
entries.description: atan2 returns the value of the arc tangent of a value expressed as a quotient of tow values: x and y. The result is expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

entries.name: ath
entries.description: This function returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent of a specified number.

entries.name: atxinfo
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: aupdate
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: CRUD Update Event.

entries.name: avg
entries.description: This function is used to compute the average value of a list of numeric elements. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: aweek
entries.description: aweek returns the first day of a week number for the year.

entries.name: b64decode
entries.description: This function decodes a base 64 string into a BLOB (Binary Large Object). It returns the number of bytes written to the BLOB. An error 26 is displayed if the input is invalid.

entries.name: b64encode
entries.description: This function encodes binary data stored in a BLOB (Binary Large Object) as a base 64 string. It returns the number of characters written to the string.

entries.name: Blbfile
entries.description: This keyword declares a binary large object (BLOB) variable.

entries.name: bookabr
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: Box
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Boxact
entries.description: Observed box activation command used in interactive screen flows.
invariants: Apply it to an explicit box identifier.

entries.name: Boxinp
entries.description: Observed boxed-input command that captures a response through a UI box.
invariants: Keep the target response variable explicit.

entries.name: Break
entries.description: This instruction is used to exit loops in scripts.

entries.name: By
entries.description: By is a keyword used in different syntaxes such as:

entries.name: Call
entries.description: This instruction is used to call a subroutine executed with its own local variables class. No value is returned by this instruction. Parameters can be transmitted as values or as references; so that the parameters transmitted by reference can be modified by the subroutine.

entries.name: callfrom
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Call ... From - calls a subroutine defined in another script.

entries.name: callmet
entries.description: This instruction invokes a method on an instance (similar to Call). It is used to invoke methods that do not return any value. For methods that return a value, use the fmeth operator.
entries.example: callmet - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Callui
entries.description: Callui is used to execute a pre-determined action on the web client.
entries.example: Callui - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Case
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: case statement.
entries.example: Case namecase}   When value}        When Default

entries.name: cast
entries.description: This function casts a generic instance to an instance of specific class or representation.

entries.name: ch
entries.description: This function returns the hyperbolic cosine of a specified number.

entries.name: char
entries.description: This keyword declares a character string with a size limited to a value that cannot exceed 255 characters.

entries.name: checkpath
entries.description: checkpath is a function that allows you to verify if a file path is in the white list of the engine and can therefore be accessed.
entries.example: checkpath - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Chgfmt
entries.description: Chgfmt changes the format of a field in a screen mask.
entries.example: Chgfmt - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Chgstl
entries.description: Chgfmt changes the format of a field in a screen mask.
entries.example: Chgstl - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Chgtbk
entries.description: Chgtbk changes the title of a block in a screen mask.
entries.example: Chgtbk - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Chgtfd
entries.description: Chgtfd changes the title of a tab associated to a mask in a window.
entries.example: Chgtfd - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Chgtzn
entries.description: Chgtzn changes the title of a field associated in a mask.
entries.example: Chgtzn - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Choose
entries.description: Choose displays a popup windows with a set of lines extracted from a table in order to select a record.
entries.example: Choose - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: chr$
entries.description: chr$ converts a numeric value into a string character having this value as a code.

entries.name: clacmp
entries.description: clacmp allows you to compare two classes (usually two classes that are associated with tables). It compares both the structure and the contents of the class.
entries.example: clacmp - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: clactxnbs
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: clactxvar
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: clalev
entries.description: clalev allows you to know if a class (usually a table associated class) can be accessed in a given context.

entries.name: clanam
entries.description: clanam allows you to know the name of a class (usually a table associated class) if available in a given context. The class name is returned with the complete class path.

entries.name: clanbs
entries.description: clanbs allows you to know the number of variables used by a class.

entries.name: clasiz
entries.description: clasiz allows you to know the size used by a class (in bytes).

entries.name: clavar
entries.description: clavar returns the name of the variables present in a class.

entries.name: Clbfile
entries.description: This keyword declares a character large object (CLOB) variable.

entries.name: close
entries.description: Close is used to free the [F] classes associated with tables and to free the cursors opened on the tables.
entries.example: close - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: closelog
entries.description: This function stops the $$PRODUCT engine log mode that opens a log file to be filled with log information. This  file is located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable.
entries.example: closelog - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: cnp
entries.description: cnp calculates the number of arrangements of 'p' objects taken from 'n'.

entries.name: columns
entries.description: Columns is used to limit the number of columns associated with a table: * Loaded in the class [F] when reading the data by a For, Readlock or Read instruction. * Updated from the class [F] when writing the data by a Rewrite or RewriteByKey instruction.
entries.example: columns - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Commit
entries.description: Commit is used to validate a database transaction.

entries.name: Const
entries.description: Const is a keyword used after a Subprog or a Func declaration to define the type of arguments transmitted to the called code.

entries.name: Continue
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: control
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Control Event.

entries.name: cop$
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: cos
entries.description: cos returns the value of the cosine of an angle expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of result is Double.

entries.name: count
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: ctrans
entries.description: ctrans transforms the characters of a string with an 8-byte code into a 7-byte code depending on a conversion table. By default, it suppresses the accents on accentuated characters. But it is also possible to define the conversion table by additional arguments.

entries.name: curr
entries.description: Curr is used as a Read or Update option to define the fact that the current record is considered.
entries.example: curr - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: currind
entries.description: currind is a numeric variable in the [G] class associated with a table declared by the File instruction. It contains the key number used during the last access to the table. When the table is declared but no read operation has been done, the default value of currind is 1.
entries.example: currind - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: currlen
entries.description: currlen is a numeric variable in the [G] class associated with a table declared by the File instruction. It can be assigned to define the number of key components used for a Read operation when the key is not mentioned and defined by currind.
entries.example: currlen - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: date
entries.description: This keyword declares a date variable where the value is within the range from [1/1/1600] to [31/12/9999], plus a null date [0/0/0].

entries.name: date$
entries.description: This function returns the current date as given from the system in a Date format. This date time is obtained from the process server and is the local time (the timezone of the server).

entries.name: datesyst
entries.description: datesyst is a system variable that has, as a default value, the current date when the $$PRODUCT process is started. It can be reassigned.
entries.example: datesyst - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: datetime
entries.description: This keyword declares a DateTime variable.

entries.name: datetime$
entries.description: This function returns the current datetime in a DateTime variable. This date time is given by default in the Greenwitch time zone and got from the process server.

entries.name: day
entries.description: day returns the day number (1 to 31) from a date.

entries.name: day$
entries.description: day$ returns a string that contains the name of the day (in the week) that corresponds to the date given as an argument. The result is given in the current connection language.

entries.name: dayn
entries.description: Dayndayn returns a number corresponding to the rank of the day in the week for the date given as an argument.

entries.name: Dbg
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Dbgaff
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: user debug break.

entries.name: dbgattach
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgbreakpointadd
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgbreakpointget
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgbreakpointremove
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgbreakpointupdate
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgcallstack
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgevalue
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dbgmode
entries.description: dbgmode is a system variable that must be different from 0 to enable the debugger to work.
entries.example: dbgmode - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: debugjava
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: user debug break for java debugger.
entries.example: If GUSER="DEBUGUSR" : Call SETDEBUGJON From DEBUGGERJ : Dbgaff : Endif

entries.name: debugyesno
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: ask for debug break.
entries.example: If GUSER="DEBUGUSR"   Local integer DEBUGRET   Call OUINON("Debug question} ?",DEBUGRET) From GESECRAN   If DEBUGRET=2

entries.name: Decimal
entries.description: This keyword declares a binary coded decimal value with up to 32 digits of precision.

entries.name: Default
entries.description: When a variable name is given without class or with a [F]class, a default priority order determines the class used for the variable. Default is used to define this search priority:

entries.name: dela
entries.description: Dela is used to delete elements from single-sized arrays, from a given index.
entries.example: dela - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Delete
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Delete - deletes the current record with lock check.

entries.name: deletebykey
entries.description: This instruction deletes a record at a given key after checking for edit conflicts. It compares the UpdTick value of the [F] record with the UpdTick of the corresponding database record and it deletes the database record only if the two ticks are equal.
entries.example: deletebykey - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: delfile
entries.description: This function deletes a file.

entries.name: desc
entries.description: This keyword is used in the Sorta instruction to describe a descending sorting order on all the parameters. See the Sorta document for more information.
entries.example: desc - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: dim
entries.description: dim returns the dimensions of a variable.
entries.example: dim - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: dimctx
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: dir$
entries.description: dir$ returns the path of the directory where the $$PRODUCT runtime engine is installed.

entries.name: Disable
entries.description: Disable allows to disable buttons and/or links present at a bottom of a screen or on the menu bar of a function.
entries.example: Disable - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Discombo
entries.description: Observed UI command that disables one or more combo choices.
invariants: Make the disabled values explicit.

entries.name: Dislbox
entries.description: Observed command that disables or closes a list-box interaction path.
invariants: Use it as part of explicit UI state transitions.

entries.name: Diszo
entries.description: Diszo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated. Unlike Grizo,Diszo stipulates that these fields have a meaning in the contex
entries.example: Diszo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Double
entries.description: This keyword declares a double precision floating value with a precision that may depend on the hardware. It is therefore **strongly recommended** not to use this keyword anymore, and to replace it by Decimal variables.

entries.name: Effzo
entries.description: Effzo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated.
entries.example: Effzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Else
entries.description: Use this keyword to define an alternative branch in an If statement. For more information, see the If documentation.

entries.name: Elsif
entries.description: Use this keyword to define an alternative condition in an If statement. For more information, see the If documentation.

entries.name: Enable
entries.description: Enable allows to enable button and/or links present at a bottom of a screen or on the menu bar of a function.
entries.example: Enable - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: End
entries.description: Use End to end execution of a routine or subprogram called by Call, Onerrgo, func, or fmet instruction.

entries.name: Endbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Endbox - displays a fatal/serious error popup.

entries.name: Endcase
entries.description: This keyword is used to end a Case statement. For more information, see the Case documentation.

entries.name: Endif
entries.description: This keyword ends an If statement. For more information, see the If documentation.

entries.name: engine-error-list
entries.description: The $$SAFE engine manages exception errors that can be handled by the Onerrgo and Resume instructions. The variable errn contains the error code according to the following table:
entries.example: engine-error-list - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: entete
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: entrypoint
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Cria um Coment�rio para Entry Point.
entries.example: entrypoint - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Envzo
entries.description: Envzo forces the display of the fields in a mask.
entries.example: Envzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: eomonth
entries.description: eomonth returns the date of the last day of the month in the year for the date given in the parameter.

entries.name: Errbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Errbox - displays an error popup.

entries.name: errl
entries.description: errl returns the number of the line in a script where an error occurred. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

entries.name: errm
entries.description: errm returns an additional error message detail when an error occurs. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

entries.name: errmes$
entries.description: errmes$ returns the message associated with a numeric error code available when an error occurs. It is frequently used when the error code errn is used, in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

entries.name: errn
entries.description: errn returns a numeric error code when an error occurs. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

entries.name: errnremote
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: errp
entries.description: errp returns the name of the script where an error occurred. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

entries.name: escjson
entries.description: This function adds the back slash (\) character in a string of character to circumvent the ones that cannot be present in a JSON constant (", \, tab, or CR).
entries.example: escjson - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: evalue
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: evalue - evaluates a formula contained in a character string.

entries.name: evaluectxvar
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: evaluesdata
entries.description: This function defines a filtering condition on a database query. The condition is expressed in SData _where_ syntax.

entries.name: Exec
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: execsql
entries.description: This instruction executes a SQL statement against the $$PRODUCT database.
entries.example: execsql - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: exp
entries.description: exp returns the exponential function of its argument.

entries.name: extended
entries.description: Use this keyword in Columns instruction as an option that describes the fact that the list of columns given as a restriction to the Select sentences sent to the database works not only for For, Rewrite, and RewriteByKey instructions, but also for Read and Readlock instructions.
entries.example: extended - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: fac
entries.description: fac returns the factorial of an integer value.

entries.name: filcom
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: file
entries.description: File is used to declare the tables that can be used in a script.
entries.example: file - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: fileabre
entries.description: fileabre is an internal character string array that gives the list of the abbreviations associated with tables that have been opened by File.
entries.example: fileabre - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: filecla
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: filelev
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: filename
entries.description: filename is an internal character string array that gives the list of the table that have been opened by File.
entries.example: filename - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: filetyp
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: filexist
entries.description: This function checks if a given $$PRODUCT script file exists on a server. It returns 1 if the file exists, if not, it returns 0.

entries.name: filinfo
entries.description: filinfo returns the properties of a file located on a file system that is accessible by the X3 engine. The file path is given as argument, and a second numeric argument defines the returned properties.

entries.name: Fillbox
entries.description: Observed command that populates a list or box widget from a source or keyed selection.
invariants: Use explicit source criteria for reproducible behavior.

entries.name: filpath
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: filpath - builds a file path on the X3 server (dir, name, ext).

entries.name: Filter
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: set restriction filter and sort order of a class.
entries.example: Filter class} Where condition} Order By orderby}

entries.name: find
entries.description: find searches for a value on a list of expressions or arrays of variables for a given type.

entries.name: first
entries.description: First is used as a read or update option to define the first record in the key order.
entries.example: first - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: fix
entries.description: fix truncates the decimals of a number.

entries.name: Float
entries.description: This keyword declares a floating value with a precision that may depend on the hardware. It is therefore **strongly recommended** not to use this keyword anymore, and to replace it with Decimal variables.

entries.name: flush
entries.description: Flush flushes the write buffer, which is filled by the Writeb instruction.
entries.example: flush - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: fmet
entries.description: This operator invokes a method on an instance (similar to func). It is used to invoke methods that do return values. For methods that do not return values, use the CallMet instruction.
entries.example: fmet - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: fmethod
entries.description: This instruction declares a method in a class. It is used to declare methods that return a value. Methods that _do not_ return any value are declared with the Method instruction.
entries.example: fmethod - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: For
entries.description: For is used to perform loops in two cases: * Read loops on database tables or joins. * Loops on index values or on a list of values.

entries.name: forfile
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: iterate over a table with hint, from, to and where clauses.
entries.example: For key} hint} From start} To end} Where restriction}    Next key}

entries.name: forlist
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: iterate over a list.

entries.name: format$
entries.description: format$ is a function that formats a value to return a formatted string value.

entries.name: formatdate
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: format$ - formats a date value into a string with pattern.

entries.name: forvar
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: iterate with a step of 1.
entries.example: For i} = start} To end}    Next i}

entries.name: FreeGroup
entries.description: This instruction frees the memory of an instance and all the instances sharing the same allocation group.

entries.name: freeheap
entries.description: The 'Freeheap' function returns the number of bytes not used in the heap memory of the runtime server process.

entries.name: freeinstance
entries.description: This instruction frees the memory of an instance. The other instances in the same allocation group are not freed; this can be done by using FreeGroup instruction.
entries.example: freeinstance - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: freemem
entries.description: The 'Freemem' function returns the number of bytes not used in the main memory of the runtime server process.

entries.name: freesnapshot
entries.description: This built-in method frees the Snapshot of an instance.
entries.example: freesnapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: From
entries.description: Use this keyword in several syntaxes with the following keywords:

entries.name: fstat
entries.description: fstat is a numeric status that is returned upon execution of a database operation, a sequential file operation, or a lock instruction.

entries.name: func
entries.description: Use this function to Call a subroutine executed with its own local variables class that returns a value. Parameters can be transmitted as values or as references; so that the parameters transmitted by reference can be modified by the subroutine.
entries.example: func - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: funcfrom
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: func ... From - calls a function defined in another script and returns a value.

entries.name: Funprog
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: funprog statement.

entries.name: gdat
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: gdat$
entries.description: gdat$ returns a date from a day, month, and year.

entries.name: gdatetime$
entries.description: This function converts a datetime string in canonical format to a DateTime value.

entries.name: GERR
entries.description: Observed global error flag used to signal that the current operation failed.
invariants: Pair it with an explanatory message when possible.

entries.name: getaccessorenabled
entries.description: This built-in property defines whether the get accessors are enabled on an instance or not.
entries.example: getaccessorenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: getbit
entries.description: getbit returns the bit value of a bit from an integer. The result is an integer that can have a value of 0 or 1.
entries.example: getbit - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: getenv$
entries.description: getenv$ allows you to read an environment variable as given in the service definition if $$PRODUCT runs on Windows, and given by assignments in the script that starts the daemon for $$PRODUCT.

entries.name: getlogname
entries.description: This function returns the file name of the last $$PRODUCT engine log. When the log mode is activated, a file located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable is opened and filled with log information.
entries.example: getlogname - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: getmodified
entries.description: This built-in method returns the list of properties that have been modified in an instance since the snapshot has been enabled.
entries.example: getmodified - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: getseq
entries.description: Getseq reads data from a binary file opened by Openi or Openio.
entries.example: getseq - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: getuuid
entries.description: getUuid generates a new UUID.

entries.name: Global
entries.description: Global variables are variables that have a global visibility and duration scope in a $$PRODUCT process.

entries.name: glossary
entries.description: The $$PRODUCT script glossary documents the language elements of the $$PRODUCT scripting engine. Every language element has an associated keyword. A keyword cannot be used as a variable or property name, whenever the case is the same or not. The keyword recognition is not case sensitive, but this will change in the future.
entries.example: glossary - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: glossary-datetime
entries.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new data type called 'DateTime'. It stores a date and an hour in the same field, while the date data type is limited to the date (usually a business date).
entries.example: glossary-datetime - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: glossary-snapshot
entries.description: A snapshot is an initial copy of the data stored in a class. It includes all child classes, if there are any. This concept is very useful when managing CRUD operation because it provides the differences between the data read and the modification done.
entries.example: glossary-snapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: glossary-structure
entries.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced structures. A structure has properties and methods and it is defined in files with an 'STC' extension.
entries.example: glossary-structure - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: glossary-updtick
entries.description: To manage optimistic update concurrency control, Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new concept called UpdTick. UpdTick, which means update ticks.
entries.example: glossary-updtick - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: glossary-uuid
entries.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new data type called UUID Universally Unique Identifier. This is a standard published by the Open Software Foundation (OSF).
entries.example: glossary-uuid - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: GMESSAGE
entries.description: Observed global message variable used to surface user-facing error or status text.
invariants: Set it alongside the matching status flag or error path.

entries.name: GOK
entries.description: Observed global success flag used to continue or abort the current workflow.
invariants: Change it intentionally because downstream flow often depends on it.

entries.name: Gosub
entries.description: Gosub is used to call a subroutine at a given label. This subroutine ends with Return and shares all the local context with the calling script. There is no parameters that can be passed and no data insulation between the script that calls and the subroutine.

entries.name: gosubfrom
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Gosub ... From - calls a subroutine label in another script.

entries.name: goto
entries.description: Goto performs an unconditional branch from a script to another label.
entries.example: goto - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: GPOINT
entries.description: Observed global entry-point variable used to select a framework hook before calling shared execution logic.
invariants: Use stable hook names because execution dispatch depends on them.

entries.name: graph$
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: Grizo
entries.description: Grizo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated.
entries.example: Grizo - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: GUSERP
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: GUSER PLUS.

entries.name: HEADER/DETAIL
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Template to create a header/detail management program, using routines from TABLEAUX.

entries.name: heapdmp
entries.description: This function generates a dump file of memory usage, for debugging purposes.
entries.example: heapdmp - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Help
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Hint
entries.description: Hint is a clause in a For instruction that accesses the database and instructs the database system the best strategy to follow to get the required data.

entries.name: Hlpbox
entries.description: Observed helper command that opens or resolves contextual help for a symbol or screen object.
invariants: Keep the help target deterministic.

entries.name: If
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: if statement.

entries.name: ifclalev
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: open local file if not already opened.
entries.example: If clalev([F:abrev}])=0 : Local File table} [abrev}] : Endif

entries.name: indcum
entries.description: indcum is a short integer variable used as a default index by the sigma function. It is only relevant when a single sigma loop is used; otherwise, you need to name the variable used as a sigma index.

entries.name: Infbox
entries.description: Infbox displays a popup windows with an information message.

entries.name: Info
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: init
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Initialization Event.

entries.name: inpbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Inpbox - displays an input popup (Classic pages).

entries.name: inpboxt
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Inpbox with Titled clause.

entries.name: inpmode
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: insa
entries.description: Insa is used to insert elements from single-sized arrays, from an index.
entries.example: insa - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Insert
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Instance
entries.description: This keyword declares an instance of a class or a representation. The instance is Null initially. You must allocate it with NewInstance before reading or writing its properties.

entries.name: instr
entries.description: instr searches for a given substring in a string, starting at a given position.

entries.name: int
entries.description: int returns the integer part of a number.

entries.name: Integer
entries.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from -2^31 to 2^31-1.

entries.name: iomode
entries.description: Use Iomode to assign a value to the different parameters associated with a sequential file opened by Openi, Openio, or Openo when doing an abbreviation.
entries.example: iomode - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: isopenadxd
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: isreadonly
entries.description: This built-in property returns whether the properties of an instance cannot or can be modified. It can be tested but tot modified.
entries.example: isreadonly - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Join
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Key
entries.description: Key is a keyword used in different syntaxes such as:

entries.name: keylen
entries.description: **Caution: keylen has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It is not to be used by developers.**
entries.example: keylen - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: keyname
entries.description: **Caution: keyname has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It has no more to be used by developers.**
entries.example: keyname - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: keyuniq
entries.description: **Caution: keyuniq has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It has no more to be used by developers.**
entries.example: keyuniq - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Kill
entries.description: Kill is an instruction that destroys variables permanently.

entries.name: label
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: label statement.

entries.name: last
entries.description: Use Last as a read or update  option to define the fact that the last record is considered (in the key order).
entries.example: last - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: left$
entries.description: left$ extracts a sub-string of a string starting from the beginning (the left side).

entries.name: len
entries.description: len returns the length (in number of characters) of a string, BLOB, or CLOB value.

entries.name: Libelle
entries.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from 0 to 255. This declaration is deprecated and should be replaced by Tinyint.

entries.name: Link
entries.description: Creates a joined alias for iterating across related tables with explicit keys.
invariants: Name the joined alias explicitly.
Prefer readable join keys over implicit coupling.
notes: Common in selection and list-style routines.

entries.name: linkfor
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Link declaration followed by a For loop on the join.

entries.name: ln
entries.description: ln returns the natural logarithm of its argument (natural means e based, where e=2.718261826459045...).

entries.name: lobsize
entries.description: lobsize returns the size (in bytes) used for the storage of BLOB or CLOB variable.

entries.name: Local
entries.description: This keyword is used as a prefix to declare local variables. Local variables have a duration and visibility scope limited to a Call, fmet, and func call.

entries.name: Local File
entries.description: Declares a local table handle for the current routine, often guarded by clalev checks in reusable code.
invariants: Avoid reopening already-open aliases.
Keep abbreviations stable inside the routine.
entries.example: If clalev([F:BPC])=0 : Local File BPCUSTOMER [BPC] : Endif
notes: Observed as a standard safety idiom in project code.

entries.name: localchar
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local Char - declares a local string variable with size.

entries.name: localdate
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local Date - declares a local date variable.

entries.name: localdec
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local Decimal - declares a local decimal (BCD) variable.

entries.name: localfile
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local File - opens a table locally for the current routine.

entries.name: localfilewhere
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local File with Where filter and Order By clause.
entries.example: Local File TABLE} [ABV}] Where condition} Order By key}

entries.name: localinst
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local Instance - declares a local instance variable with class type.

entries.name: localint
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Local Integer - declares a local integer variable.

entries.name: Lock
entries.description: Lock is used to lock either a symbol or a table.

entries.name: lockwait
entries.description: lockwait is an Integer system variable of the Integer type.  It allows you to set the maximum of seconds that each lock attempt will last using the Lock and Readlock instructions. After a first failed lock attempt, the system will retry every second for the duration defined by the lockwait value.
entries.example: lockwait - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: log
entries.description: log returns the 10 based logarithm of its argument.

entries.name: logicclose
entries.description: This function closes a table logically. It releases the [F] buffer and any filters in a way that makes a further Local File execution much faster than if the table were closed by the Close instruction.
entries.example: logicclose - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Look
entries.description: Look can be used to check the existence of data in the database for a condition. It behaves exactly as the Read operations and has the same syntax. The only differences are the following: * The variables present in the [F] class are not modified. * Look cannot be used on joins declared with the Link instruction. * Look does not allow you to know if the record is locked. The only solution is to check the user Readlock.

entries.name: Mask
entries.description: Mask is used to declare all the masks that can be used in a routine.

entries.name: maskabr
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: maskcla
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: maskcou
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: masklev
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: masknam
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: masknbf
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: maskrk
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: masksiz
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: max
entries.description: This function is used to calculate the maximum for a list of elements such as dates, numeric values, and string values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: maxheap
entries.description: The maxheap variable defines the memory (in bytes) that can be allocated in the heap memory of the runtime server process.
entries.example: maxheap - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: maxmem
entries.description: The maxmem variable defines the memory (in bytes) that can be allocated in the main memory of the runtime server process.
entries.example: maxmem - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: maxtab
entries.description: This function returns the maximum index used for the first dimension of an array.
entries.example: maxtab - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Menu
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Mesbox
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: mess
entries.description: mess gives access to the translatable text resources used by the $$SAFE engine and the application layers (especially local menus and identified messages such as error messages).

entries.name: method
entries.description: This instruction declares a method in a class. It is used to declare methods that **_do not_** return any value. Methods that **_do_** return a value are declared with the Fmethod instruction.
entries.example: method - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: mid$
entries.description: mid$ extracts a sub-string defined by the position and a number of characters out of a CLOB or a string.

entries.name: min
entries.description: This function is used to calculate the minimum for a list of elements such as dates, numeric values, or string values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: mkstat
entries.description: Observed runtime mask status variable assigned during validation or interaction flow.
invariants: Set it deliberately because it changes UI behavior.

entries.name: mod
entries.description: mod returns the remainder of the division of one number by another.

entries.name: modified
entries.description: This attribute returns whether a property of an instance has been modified since the snapshot has been enabled on the instance.
entries.example: modified - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: month
entries.description: month extracts the month number (1 to 12) from a date.

entries.name: month$
entries.description: month$ returns the month name in the current language from a date.

entries.name: nbind
entries.description: nbind returns the number of indexes in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
entries.example: nbind - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: nbrecord
entries.description: nbrecord returns the number of lines in a database table.

entries.name: nbruser
entries.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

entries.name: nbzon
entries.description: nbzon returns the number of columns in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
entries.example: nbzon - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: nday
entries.description: nday returns the number of days between the [1/1/1600] and the date given as the argument.

entries.name: nday$
entries.description: nday$ returns a date from the number of days between the [1/1/1600] and the date.

entries.name: NewInstance
entries.description: This instruction allocates a class or representation instance.

entries.name: Next
entries.description: Use Next in the following two cases: * To end loops. See the Next loop documentation. * As a read or update option. See the Next read documentation.

entries.name: next-loop
entries.description: Use Next in For loops to end the loop.
entries.example: next-loop - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: next-read
entries.description: Use Next as a read or update option to define the fact that the next record is considered (in the key order).
entries.example: next-read - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: nohint
entries.description: Use Nohint in the following clause:
entries.example: nohint - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Nolign
entries.description: nolign is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives the current line value (starting at 1). It is also used in class assignment to identify the current line when a grid in a mask class is assigned from or to a table class.
entries.example: Nolign - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Nolign1
entries.description: nolign1 is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives the range end on operation involving a range of lines (nolign is the starting point of the range).
entries.example: Nolign1 - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: nomap
entries.description: Nomap is a character string type variable array that contains the names of the current folder.
entries.example: nomap - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: not
entries.description: not corresponds to the unary boolean 'not' operator that can also be abbreviated by !.
entries.example: not - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: null
entries.description: This keyword represents the **_null_** instance.

entries.name: nulluuid
entries.description: This keyword represents the **_null_** UUID constant.

entries.name: num
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: num$
entries.description: num$ transforms any data type (except BLOBs, CLOBs, and instance pointers) into a string that contains the representation of the data.

entries.name: objectexist
entries.description: This function checks if a given class exists on an application server.

entries.name: objectnbs
entries.description: This built-in property returns the number of properties of an instance.
entries.example: objectnbs - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: objecttype
entries.description: This built-in property returns the name of the class or representation of an instance.
entries.example: objecttype - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: objectvar
entries.description: This keyword gives access to the Nth properties of the class associated with a given instance ('N' given as a parameter). It might be important if a pointer to a class is sent to a program that will explore the properties of the class.
entries.example: objectvar - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Onerrgo
entries.description: Declares an error label and redirects runtime errors to an explicit recovery or resume path.
invariants: Keep the error label close to the routine.
Resume only when the failure path is understood.
notes: Useful for legacy-style guarded sections, but should remain explicit.

entries.name: onerrgom
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Onerrgo - error handler with error message capture (errn, errl, errp, errmes$).

entries.name: Onevent
entries.description: Observed UI event-binding construct that routes screen events to handlers.
invariants: Keep the event name explicit.
Route to a stable label or routine.
entries.example: Onevent CONTROL Call MY_CONTROL From MYSCRIPT

entries.name: Onintgo
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Onkey
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Opened
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: openi
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Openi - opens a file for reading with Rdseq loop.
entries.example: Openi filepath},0 Repeat   Rdseq BUFFER}   If fstat=0

entries.name: openio
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Openio - opens a file for reading and writing.

entries.name: openiusing
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Openi with Using abbreviation - read loop with explicit file handle.
entries.example: Openi filepath},0 Using [ABV}] Repeat   Rdseq BUFFER} Using [ABV}]   If fstat=0

entries.name: openlog
entries.description: This function sets the $$PRODUCT engine in log mode. When log mode is activated, a file located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable is opened and filled with log information.
entries.example: openlog - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: openo
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Openo - opens a file for writing with Wrseq.

entries.name: or
entries.description: or performs a logical 'or' between two boolean values.
entries.example: or - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Order
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: order-by
entries.description: Order By is a clause that can be used in conjunction with several language keywords:
entries.example: order-by - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: parse
entries.description: parse parses a formula contained in a character string and returns an error value if the formula is not syntactically correct.

entries.name: pat
entries.description: pat allows you to check the conformity of a string according to a pattern.

entries.name: pcolor
entries.description: Observed runtime color state variable used in UI rendering or highlighting.
invariants: Treat it as presentation state, not domain data.

entries.name: pi
entries.description: pi returns an approximated value of the 𝜋 (pi) number.

entries.name: Pickbox
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: prd
entries.description: This function is used to calculate the multiplication for a list of numeric elements. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: prev
entries.description: Prev is used as a read or update option to define that the previous record is considered in the key order.
entries.example: prev - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: PRINTPARAM
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: REPORT : When used in the IMPRIME subprog, put in a trace file the list of parameters sent to CR.
entries.example: Local Char PARAM} (50), VALEUR} (50) Local Integer I}  Local Integer PARAM}(250)(50), VALEUR}(250)(50)

entries.name: progcan
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: progldd
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: progsiz
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: progusd
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: propagate
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Propagate Event.

entries.name: putseq
entries.description: Putseq writes data on a binary file opened by Openo or Openio.
entries.example: putseq - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Qstbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Qstbox - yes/no question popup using OUINON from GESECRAN.
entries.example: Local Integer RESULT} Call OUINON("question}",RESULT}) From GESECRAN If RESULT}=2

entries.name: Raz
entries.description: Raz allows you to set variables or variable classes to null values.

entries.name: rdseq
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Rdseq - reads a line from a sequential file.

entries.name: Read
entries.description: Use Read to get data from a table or a join through a SQL select instruction based on the value of an index key previously defined or a temporary index.

entries.name: readequalkey
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: read equal key statement.
entries.example: Read [F:class}]index}=values} If fstat=1   lock_statements} Elsif fstat

entries.name: readlock
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Readlock - reads a record with an exclusive lock.

entries.name: reckey
entries.description: reckey is a pseudo key present in database table using this key ensures that the sequential reading order will be optimal (it uses the physical order of the database and thus avoids any data sort when the query is sent to the database). It can be used in For loops.
entries.example: reckey - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: renamefile
entries.description: This function renames a file.

entries.name: Repeat
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Repeat ... Until - loop until a condition is fulfilled.

entries.name: Report
entries.description: Observed reporting command used to launch a report with parameters and capture a return value.
invariants: Keep the target and parameter set explicit.

entries.name: resume
entries.description: Resume allows you to end the execution of an error handling routine and resume the execution at the line that follows the line where the error was thrown.
entries.example: resume - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Return
entries.description: Return ends a script called by Gosub and resumes the execution to the instruction that follows the Gosub.

entries.name: reverttosnapshot
entries.description: This built-in method reverts an instance to its Snapshot state.
entries.example: reverttosnapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: revertupdtick
entries.description: This built-in method reverts the updtick value of an instance to its snapshot value.
entries.example: revertupdtick - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Rewrite
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Rewrite - rewrites the current record with fstat check.

entries.name: rewritebykey
entries.description: This instruction updates a record at a given key after checking for edit conflicts. It compares the Updtick value of the [F] record with the updtick value of the corresponding database record, and it updates the database record only if the two ticks are equal.
entries.example: rewritebykey - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: right$
entries.description: right$ extracts a substring of a string starting from a given position (the right side).

entries.name: rnd
entries.description: rnd(x) returns a random number between '0' and 'x' (x excluded).

entries.name: Rollback
entries.description: Rollback is used to end a database transaction and comes back to the situation before the beginning of the transaction (Trbegin).

entries.name: rowcount
entries.description: rowcount allows you to return the number of lines corresponding to a selection condition in a database table.

entries.name: sadmem
entries.description: The sadmem variable defines the memory (in KB) that is allocated in database access processes as buffers.
entries.example: sadmem - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Saizo
entries.description: Observed screen-layout command that resizes or adjusts a mask area or field zone.
invariants: Use it carefully because it changes layout behavior.

entries.name: schar
entries.description: This keyword declares a character string with character coded on 1 byte (with a size limited to a value that cannot exceed 255 characters). It is strongly recommended to use Char declaration to avoid issues when dealing with texts that can contains Unicode characters.
entries.example: schar - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: seek
entries.description: Seek allows you to move the read pointer on a file opened by Openi or Openio. When a null value is sent, it also allows you to flush the write operation performed on a file opened by Openo or Openio.
entries.example: seek - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: seg
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: seg$
entries.description: seg$ extracts a substring defined by the starting position and the ending position out of a CLOB or a string.

entries.name: Selbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Selbox - displays a selection popup with multiple choices.

entries.name: SELECTION FROM LIST
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Selection from list template.

entries.name: SELECTION FROM TABLE
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: selection from table, without object - STILL NEED TO ADAPT IT TO YOUR NEED THOUGH.

entries.name: setaccessorenabled
entries.description: This built-in property defines whether the set accessors are enabled on an instance.
entries.example: setaccessorenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: setbit
entries.description: setbit set a bit of an integer to 0 or 1.  The result is an integer.
entries.example: setbit - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Setblb
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Setclb
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Setinstance
entries.description: This instruction performs an assignment between the properties of a class instance and the content of a file class or a mask class (for example, an [F:xxxx] table buffer or [M:yyyy] mask class), in one direction or the other.

entries.name: setinstancenosys
entries.description: This instruction performs an assignment between the properties of a class instance and the content of a file class or a mask class (for example, an [F:xxxx] table buffer or [M:yyyy] mask class), in one direction or the other. It works exactly as setinstance, but it excludes technical properties from the assignment.
entries.example: setinstancenosys - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Setlbox
entries.description: Observed command that sets the active list box or list-box selection context.
invariants: Keep the target list box explicit.

entries.name: Setlob
entries.description: Setlob allows you to assign a CLOB data with a string variable array and vice versa.

entries.name: Setmode
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Setmok
entries.description: Observed mask-state command used to change editability or interaction state.
invariants: Use explicit mode values.
Apply it to the intended mask scope.

entries.name: Setparam
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: sgn
entries.description: sgn returns the sign (-1, 0 or +1) of a numeric value.

entries.name: sh
entries.description: This function returns the hyperbolic sine of a specified number.

entries.name: Shortint
entries.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from -32768 to 32767.

entries.name: sigma
entries.description: sigma allows you to perform the sum of a series of numeric expressions and the concatenation of a series of expressions returning strings. These expressions depend on an index value that varies between two values with a step of 1.

entries.name: sin
entries.description: Sin returns the value of the sine of an angle expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

entries.name: Sleep
entries.description: Sleep allows you to pause the execution of a script for a given number of seconds.

entries.name: snapshot
entries.description: This built-in method gives access to the Snapshot image of an instance.
entries.example: snapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: snapshotenabled
entries.description: This property of a class defines whether the snapshot is enabled on the class (if equal to 1) or disabled (if equal to 0).
entries.example: snapshotenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Sorta
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Sorta - sorts a list of parallel arrays.

entries.name: space$
entries.description: space$ returns a string containing a number of spaces given by its argument.

entries.name: sql
entries.description: Sql allows you to execute a Select statement in the database.
entries.example: sql - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: sqr
entries.description: sqr returns the square root of a number.

entries.name: stability
entries.description: Stability is a keyword used in a For loop. It is an option dedicated to SQL server that allows the For loop to see only the lines that were in the database when the For loop was started.
entries.example: stability - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: stat1
entries.description: stat1 is a system variable that contains the number of lines returned by a System instruction.
entries.example: stat1 - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: status
entries.description: status is a system variable that contains the return status of an entry statement.

entries.name: Step
entries.description: Step is a keyword used in the For loop syntax to give an increment added to the loop variable on every loop.

entries.name: str
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: strdecode
entries.description: This function transforms a UTF8 or ASCII encoded character string (based on the parameter given) into a UCS2 format. It returns the number of character found in the destination.

entries.name: strencode
entries.description: This function transforms a UCS2 encoded character string into another format that is given by an additional parameter. It returns the number of character found in the destination.

entries.name: string$
entries.description: string$ returns a string containing a string repeated a given number of  times.

entries.name: Subprog
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: subprog statement.

entries.name: sum
entries.description: This function is used to perform an addition of numeric elements or a concatenation of string elements. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: System
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: System - executes a system/OS command.

entries.name: Table
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: tairec
entries.description: tairec returns the size of a record (line) in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
entries.example: tairec - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: tan
entries.description: tan returns the value of the tangent of an angle expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of result is Double.

entries.name: th
entries.description: This function returns the hyperbolic tangent of a specified number.

entries.name: then
entries.description: Then is a keyword that can be used between the condition in the If statement and the next statement. It is completely optional: an instruction separator such as the colon or new line is also available.
entries.example: then - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: this
entries.description: This function gives access to the class instance pointer when executing a method on a class or a property of a class.
entries.example: this - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: time
entries.description: time returns the local time of the current process server. The value returned is the number of seconds elapsed since midnight.

entries.name: time$
entries.description: This function returns the current time, as given from the system, as a string using the hh:mm:ss format. This time is obtained from the process server and is the local time (in the timezone of the server).

entries.name: timestamp$
entries.description: timestamp$ returns a string value containing a time stamp, which is the number of milliseconds elapsed since the first of January 1970, at 00:00:00 time GMT.

entries.name: Tinyint
entries.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from 0 to 255.

entries.name: Titcol
