entries.description: Titcol allows to assign dynamically titles in the columns of a grid present on a screen.
entries.example: Titcol - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Title
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: Titled
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: To
entries.description: To is used in the For syntax to indicate the limit of a loop value. It was also used by syntaxes that are now deprecated.

entries.name: tolower
entries.description: tolower transforms the upper case letters in a string into lower case letters. The other characters remain unchanged.

entries.name: tosdata
entries.description: This function returns a string value containing an SData _where_ syntax corresponding to a $$PRODUCT valid condition.
entries.example: tosdata - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: toupper
entries.description: toupper converts the lower case letters in a string into upper case letters. Other characters remain unchanged.

entries.name: touuid
entries.description: This keyword converts a canonical UUID string to its UUID value.

entries.name: traceclose
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: close the current trace.
entries.example: If !GSERVEUR : Call FERME_TRACE() From LECFIC : Endif

entries.name: traceopen
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: open a new trace.
entries.example: If !GSERVEUR : Call OUVRE_TRACE(title}) From LECFIC : Endif

entries.name: traceread
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: read from the current trace.

entries.name: tracewrite
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: write in the current trace.

entries.name: Transmask
entries.description: Observed command that transfers values between mask contexts.
invariants: Keep source and target masks explicit.

entries.name: Trbegin
entries.description: Starts an explicit transaction block that should end with Commit or Rollback after checking fstat.
invariants: Always pair with explicit error handling.
Do not leave transaction outcome implicit.
entries.example: Trbegin [BPC] / Rewrite [BPC] / If fstat : Rollback : Else : Commit : Endif
notes: Promoted by snippets and aligned with explicitness requirements.

entries.name: trbegina
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Trbegin/Commit/Rollback with table abbreviation.

entries.name: Treebox
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: trtcou
entries.description: trtcou returns the name of the current script.

entries.name: type
entries.description: type returns an integer value representing the data type of a variable.

entries.name: typectx
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: unescjson
entries.description: This function restores a JSON character string to its original state. It removes back slash (\) characters that the  Escjson function would have put in place to enable getting incompatible characters in a JSON constant (", \, tab, or CR).
entries.example: unescjson - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: uni
entries.description: uni allows you to verify if there are duplicated values in a list of variables or expressions that can have any type (numeric, string CLOB, dates, UUIDs, or datetime).

entries.name: uniqid
entries.description: uniqid allows you to generate a unique series of long integer for every database table.

entries.name: Unlock
entries.description: Unlock allows to unlock either a symbol or all the tables previously locked by the development partner.

entries.name: Until
entries.description: Until ends a Repeat loop.

entries.name: Update
entries.description: Update allows you to update a list of database lines defined by a Where condition with values transmitted as parameters.

entries.name: updtick
entries.description: This technical column is added in every database table when the table is validated in version 7 for the supervisor. This column stores a revision number for every line in the database. The revision numbers start at 1 when a line is created, and increment by 1 every time an update is made through a database trigger.

entries.name: Using
entries.description: Using is a keyword used in different syntaxes, especially for the following statements: * Openi, Openo, Iomode, Rdseq, Wrseq, Getseq, and Putseq to indicate an abbreviation when managing a sequential file. * Instance to indicate the class to be used when an instance must be created.

entries.name: uuid$
entries.description: uuid$ generates a new canconical (string) UUID.

entries.name: Uuident
entries.description: This keyword declares a UUID variable.

entries.name: val
entries.description: val transforms a string or a CLOB value that contains a decimal representation of a number to this number.

entries.name: Value
entries.description: Value is a keyword used after a Subprog or a Func declaration, to define the type of arguments transmitted to the called code.

entries.name: var
entries.description: Use var to calculate the variance of a series of numeric values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or subarrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

entries.name: Variable
entries.description: Variable is a keyword used after a Subprog or a Func declaration, to define the type of arguments transmitted to the called code.

entries.name: ver$
entries.description: ver$(0) returns the revision number of the $$SAFE engine. The type of result is Char.

entries.name: vireblc
entries.description: vireblc allows you to suppress leading, trailing, or internal spaces of a string character or CLOB expression.

entries.name: Wait
entries.description: Core 4GL keyword used to control execution flow, data access, UI behavior, or runtime directives.

entries.name: week
entries.description: week returns the day number (0 to 53) from a date.

entries.name: Wend
entries.description: Wend ends a While loop of instructions.

entries.name: When
entries.description: When is the keyword that indicates a branch in a Case alternative. The syntax is the following:

entries.name: Where
entries.description: Use Where to add a filter:  * In a table declared by File.  * On a join declared by Link.  * On an update triggered by Update or Delete.  * On a read loop executed with For.

entries.name: While
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: While ... Wend - loop while a condition is true.

entries.name: With
entries.description: With is a keyword used in several syntaxes: * It separates the element to assign and the expression that gives its value for Assign, Setlob, Setinstance. * It adds some options in For loops based on database cursors (With Lock, WithStability, and so forth). * It gives a waiting time in Lock With lockwait=... syntax. * it separates the main table description from the table in which joins are done in Link ... with... syntax. * it separates the main table description from the column assignments in Update ... with... syntax.

entries.name: Write
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Write - inserts a new record with fstat check.

entries.name: writeb
entries.description: Writeb has the same syntax as Write, but allows you to optimize the performances when several insertions of database lines must be done.
entries.example: writeb - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Wrnbox
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Wrnbox - displays a warning popup.

entries.name: wrnboxt
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Wrnbox with Titled clause.

entries.name: wrseq
entries.description: Snippet-backed pattern: Wrseq - writes a line to a sequential file.

entries.name: x3script-keywords-glossary
entries.description: **The $$PRODUCT scripting language includes a list of keywords. Not all these keywords are used, but they remain reserved words in the language.**
entries.example: x3script-keywords-glossary - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: xcrypt
entries.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

entries.name: xgetchar
entries.description: This function extracts the Nth character of a CHAR or CLOB variable. This function runs much faster than the usual functions such as mid$ or seg$ when managing large CLOB data.
entries.example: xgetchar - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: xor
entries.description: xor performs an exclusive logical 'or' between two boolean values.
entries.example: xor - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: year
entries.description: year returns the year number (1600 to 9999) from a date.

entries.name: Zc
entries.description: zc is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives access to the current value during controls, initialization, selection and help routines.
entries.example: Zc - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Zoncou
entries.description: zoncou is a system variable that contains the name of the current field during a control called when an input in a mask is performed.
entries.example: Zoncou - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Zonsor
entries.description: zonsor is a system variable that contains the name of the last field entered when an input in a mask is performed.
entries.example: Zonsor - see category notes and syntax guide.

entries.name: Zonsui
entries.description: zonsui is a system variable that can be assigned during a control called during an input in a mask in order to change the order of input by reassigning the next field to be inputted.
entries.example: Zonsui - see category notes and syntax guide.

function.name: arr

function.name: ascii
function.description: ascii returns the code of the first character of a string.

function.name: ash

function.name: asin

function.name: aweek
function.description: aweek returns the first day of a week number for the year.

function.name: b64encode

function.name: day

function.name: evalue

function.name: evaluectxvar
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: evaluesdata

function.name: exp

function.name: fac

function.name: filpath

function.name: gdat$

function.name: gdatetime$
function.description: This function converts a datetime string in canonical format to a DateTime value.

function.name: getenv$

function.name: graph$
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: int

function.name: isopenadxd

function.name: left$

function.name: len

function.name: ln

function.name: lobsize

function.name: log

function.name: masksiz
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: max
function.description: This function is used to calculate the maximum for a list of elements such as dates, numeric values, and string values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

function.name: mess
function.description: mess gives access to the translatable text resources used by the $$SAFE engine and the application layers (especially local menus and identified messages such as error messages).

function.name: mid$
function.description: mid$ extracts a sub-string defined by the position and a number of characters out of a CLOB or a string.

function.name: min
function.description: This function is used to calculate the minimum for a list of elements such as dates, numeric values, or string values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

function.name: mod
function.description: mod returns the remainder of the division of one number by another.

function.name: month
function.description: month extracts the month number (1 to 12) from a date.

function.name: month$
function.description: month$ returns the month name in the current language from a date.

function.name: nbrecord
function.description: nbrecord returns the number of lines in a database table.

function.name: nday
function.description: nday returns the number of days between the [1/1/1600] and the date given as the argument.

function.name: nday$
function.description: nday$ returns a date from the number of days between the [1/1/1600] and the date.

function.name: num
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: num$
function.description: num$ transforms any data type (except BLOBs, CLOBs, and instance pointers) into a string that contains the representation of the data.

function.name: objectexist
function.description: This function checks if a given class exists on an application server.

function.name: parse
function.description: parse parses a formula contained in a character string and returns an error value if the formula is not syntactically correct.

function.name: pat
function.description: pat allows you to check the conformity of a string according to a pattern.

function.name: prd
function.description: This function is used to calculate the multiplication for a list of numeric elements. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

function.name: progcan
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: progldd
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: progsiz
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: progusd
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: renamefile
function.description: This function renames a file.

function.name: right$
function.description: right$ extracts a substring of a string starting from a given position (the right side).

function.name: rnd
function.description: rnd(x) returns a random number between '0' and 'x' (x excluded).

function.name: rowcount
function.description: rowcount allows you to return the number of lines corresponding to a selection condition in a database table.

function.name: seg
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: seg$
function.description: seg$ extracts a substring defined by the starting position and the ending position out of a CLOB or a string.

function.name: sgn
function.description: sgn returns the sign (-1, 0 or +1) of a numeric value.

function.name: sh
function.description: This function returns the hyperbolic sine of a specified number.

function.name: sigma
function.description: sigma allows you to perform the sum of a series of numeric expressions and the concatenation of a series of expressions returning strings. These expressions depend on an index value that varies between two values with a step of 1.

function.name: sin
function.description: Sin returns the value of the sine of an angle expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of the result is Double.

function.name: space$
function.description: space$ returns a string containing a number of spaces given by its argument.

function.name: sqr
function.description: sqr returns the square root of a number.

function.name: str
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: strdecode
function.description: This function transforms a UTF8 or ASCII encoded character string (based on the parameter given) into a UCS2 format. It returns the number of character found in the destination.

function.name: strencode
function.description: This function transforms a UCS2 encoded character string into another format that is given by an additional parameter. It returns the number of character found in the destination.

function.name: string$
function.description: string$ returns a string containing a string repeated a given number of  times.

function.name: sum
function.description: This function is used to perform an addition of numeric elements or a concatenation of string elements. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or sub-arrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

function.name: tan
function.description: tan returns the value of the tangent of an angle expressed in degrees, radians, or grades, depending on adxmda. The type of result is Double.

function.name: th
function.description: This function returns the hyperbolic tangent of a specified number.

function.name: tolower
function.description: tolower transforms the upper case letters in a string into lower case letters. The other characters remain unchanged.

function.name: toupper
function.description: toupper converts the lower case letters in a string into upper case letters. Other characters remain unchanged.

function.name: touuid
function.description: This keyword converts a canonical UUID string to its UUID value.

function.name: type
function.description: type returns an integer value representing the data type of a variable.

function.name: typectx
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: uni
function.description: uni allows you to verify if there are duplicated values in a list of variables or expressions that can have any type (numeric, string CLOB, dates, UUIDs, or datetime).

function.name: uniqid
function.description: uniqid allows you to generate a unique series of long integer for every database table.

function.name: updtick
function.description: This technical column is added in every database table when the table is validated in version 7 for the supervisor. This column stores a revision number for every line in the database. The revision numbers start at 1 when a line is created, and increment by 1 every time an update is made through a database trigger.

function.name: val
function.description: val transforms a string or a CLOB value that contains a decimal representation of a number to this number.

function.name: var
function.description: Use var to calculate the variance of a series of numeric values. It works on a list of elements that can be: * Expressions. * Arrays or subarrays of an array. * Properties in an array of references.

function.name: ver$
function.description: ver$(0) returns the revision number of the $$SAFE engine. The type of result is Char.

function.name: vireblc
function.description: vireblc allows you to suppress leading, trailing, or internal spaces of a string character or CLOB expression.

function.name: week
function.description: week returns the day number (0 to 53) from a date.

function.name: xcrypt
function.description: Built-in 4GL function available to compute values, inspect runtime state, or transform data.

function.name: year
function.description: year returns the year number (1600 to 9999) from a date.

event-pattern.name: ACTION
event-pattern.description: Entry dispatch block frequently used to route object or screen actions to Gosub labels.
invariants: Return after the Case block.
Keep labels explicit and action names stable.
event-pattern.example: $ACTION / Case ACTION / When "OUVRE" : Gosub OUVRE / Endcase / Return
notes: Strongly represented in snippets and corpus.

help-topic.name: Actzo
help-topic.description: Actzo activates all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously grayed.
help-topic.example: Actzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxdcs
help-topic.description: adxdcs is a pivot year used to determine the century when the year is two digits.
help-topic.example: adxdcs - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxdir
help-topic.description: This function returns the path of the directory where the $$PRODUCT execution engine run-time is installed on the current process server.
help-topic.example: adxdir - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxdlrec
help-topic.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last Delete instruction. It must be called after the Delete instruction.
help-topic.example: adxdlrec - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxfname
help-topic.description: adxfname returns the name of the columns of an opened table. It is an array of string variables present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the **_abv_** abbreviation.
help-topic.example: adxfname - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxftl
help-topic.description: adxftl is a variable used to set a read grouping factor in a number of records.
help-topic.example: adxftl - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxifs
help-topic.description: adxifs is a character string variable of length 1 that contains the field separator used when reading or writing sequential files by the instructions Rdseq and Wrseq.
help-topic.example: adxifs - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxirs
help-topic.description: adxirs is a character string variable of length 2 maximum that contains the record separator used when reading or writing sequential files by the instructions Rdseq and Wrseq.
help-topic.example: adxirs - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxium
help-topic.description: Adxium is a numeric value that defines the coding method used for writing or reading character strings in a text file by the instructions  Wrseq and Rdseq.
help-topic.example: adxium - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxlog
help-topic.description: adxlog is a variable that states if a database transaction has already been started (by Trbegin).
help-topic.example: adxlog - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxmda
help-topic.description: adxmda is a numeric value that determines the current angular mode. It is used for the functions that return or use angles.
help-topic.example: adxmda - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxmother
help-topic.description: Adxmother is a character string type variable array that contains the names of the reference folder of the current folder.
help-topic.example: adxmother - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxmso
help-topic.description: adxmso is an internal integer variable that indicates the maximum number of files that can be simultaneously opened by Openi, Openo, and Openio.
help-topic.example: adxmso - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxmto
help-topic.description: adxmto is an internal integer variable that indicates the maximum number of tables that can be simultaneously opened by File.
help-topic.example: adxmto - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxsca
help-topic.description: adxsca is an internal string variable that stores characters used by formatting functions.
help-topic.example: adxsca - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxsqlrec
help-topic.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last ExecSql instruction. It must be called after an ExecSql instruction that inserts, updates, or deletes records. This function returns 0 after ExecSql instructions that execute DDL statements like CREATE TABLE or DROP TABLE.
help-topic.example: adxsqlrec - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtct
help-topic.description: adxtct is a Char variable that indicates the name of the table where the sequence numbers are stored.
help-topic.example: adxtct - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtlk
help-topic.description: adxtlk is a Char variable. It defines the name of the table that stores the logical locks.
help-topic.example: adxtlk - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtms
help-topic.description: adxtms is a Char variable that defines the name of the table where the messages are stored.
help-topic.example: adxtms - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtuc
help-topic.description: adxtuc is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 33. It allows you to define accepted characters in additional data types used for formatting data.
help-topic.example: adxtuc - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtul
help-topic.description: adxtul is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 1. It allows you to define data types used for formatting data.
help-topic.example: adxtul - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxtut
help-topic.description: adxtut is an array of 32 character strings (from 0 to 31) with a maximum length of 17. It allows you to define sub-data types associated with additional data types used for formatting data.
help-topic.example: adxtut - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxuprec
help-topic.description: This function returns the number of records processed by the last Update instruction, and it must be called after the instruction.
help-topic.example: adxuprec - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: adxwrb
help-topic.description: adxwrb stores the number of buffered database write operations.
help-topic.example: adxwrb - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Affzo
help-topic.description: Affzo displays all or part of the fields in a mask.
help-topic.example: Affzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: allocgrp
help-topic.description: This built-in property returns the allocation group of an instance. Allocgrp is essentially a technical property and is rarely used.
help-topic.example: allocgrp - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: anasql
help-topic.description: Anasql is used to parse a SQL sentence and return in an integer array the number and the types of values that are returned by the SQL sentence.
help-topic.example: anasql - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: and
help-topic.description: and performs a logical 'and' between two boolean values.
help-topic.example: and - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: asc
help-topic.description: This keyword is used in the Sorta instruction to describe an ascending sorting order on all parameters. See the Sorta for more information.
help-topic.example: asc - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: callmet
help-topic.description: This instruction invokes a method on an instance (similar to Call). It is used to invoke methods that do not return any value. For methods that return a value, use the fmeth operator.
help-topic.example: callmet - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Callui
help-topic.description: Callui is used to execute a pre-determined action on the web client.
help-topic.example: Callui - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: checkpath
help-topic.description: checkpath is a function that allows you to verify if a file path is in the white list of the engine and can therefore be accessed.
help-topic.example: checkpath - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Chgfmt
help-topic.description: Chgfmt changes the format of a field in a screen mask.
help-topic.example: Chgfmt - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Chgstl
help-topic.description: Chgfmt changes the format of a field in a screen mask.
help-topic.example: Chgstl - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Chgtbk
help-topic.description: Chgtbk changes the title of a block in a screen mask.
help-topic.example: Chgtbk - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Chgtfd
help-topic.description: Chgtfd changes the title of a tab associated to a mask in a window.
help-topic.example: Chgtfd - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Chgtzn
help-topic.description: Chgtzn changes the title of a field associated in a mask.
help-topic.example: Chgtzn - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Choose
help-topic.description: Choose displays a popup windows with a set of lines extracted from a table in order to select a record.
help-topic.example: Choose - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: clacmp
help-topic.description: clacmp allows you to compare two classes (usually two classes that are associated with tables). It compares both the structure and the contents of the class.
help-topic.example: clacmp - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: close
help-topic.description: Close is used to free the [F] classes associated with tables and to free the cursors opened on the tables.
help-topic.example: close - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: closelog
help-topic.description: This function stops the $$PRODUCT engine log mode that opens a log file to be filled with log information. This  file is located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable.
help-topic.example: closelog - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: columns
help-topic.description: Columns is used to limit the number of columns associated with a table: * Loaded in the class [F] when reading the data by a For, Readlock or Read instruction. * Updated from the class [F] when writing the data by a Rewrite or RewriteByKey instruction.
help-topic.example: columns - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: curr
help-topic.description: Curr is used as a Read or Update option to define the fact that the current record is considered.
help-topic.example: curr - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: currind
help-topic.description: currind is a numeric variable in the [G] class associated with a table declared by the File instruction. It contains the key number used during the last access to the table. When the table is declared but no read operation has been done, the default value of currind is 1.
help-topic.example: currind - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: currlen
help-topic.description: currlen is a numeric variable in the [G] class associated with a table declared by the File instruction. It can be assigned to define the number of key components used for a Read operation when the key is not mentioned and defined by currind.
help-topic.example: currlen - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: datesyst
help-topic.description: datesyst is a system variable that has, as a default value, the current date when the $$PRODUCT process is started. It can be reassigned.
help-topic.example: datesyst - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: dbgmode
help-topic.description: dbgmode is a system variable that must be different from 0 to enable the debugger to work.
help-topic.example: dbgmode - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: dela
help-topic.description: Dela is used to delete elements from single-sized arrays, from a given index.
help-topic.example: dela - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: deletebykey
help-topic.description: This instruction deletes a record at a given key after checking for edit conflicts. It compares the UpdTick value of the [F] record with the UpdTick of the corresponding database record and it deletes the database record only if the two ticks are equal.
help-topic.example: deletebykey - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: desc
help-topic.description: This keyword is used in the Sorta instruction to describe a descending sorting order on all the parameters. See the Sorta document for more information.
help-topic.example: desc - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: dim
help-topic.description: dim returns the dimensions of a variable.
help-topic.example: dim - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Disable
help-topic.description: Disable allows to disable buttons and/or links present at a bottom of a screen or on the menu bar of a function.
help-topic.example: Disable - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Diszo
help-topic.description: Diszo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated. Unlike Grizo,Diszo stipulates that these fields have a meaning in the contex
help-topic.example: Diszo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Effzo
help-topic.description: Effzo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated.
help-topic.example: Effzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Enable
help-topic.description: Enable allows to enable button and/or links present at a bottom of a screen or on the menu bar of a function.
help-topic.example: Enable - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: engine-error-list
help-topic.description: The $$SAFE engine manages exception errors that can be handled by the Onerrgo and Resume instructions. The variable errn contains the error code according to the following table:
help-topic.example: engine-error-list - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Envzo
help-topic.description: Envzo forces the display of the fields in a mask.
help-topic.example: Envzo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: escjson
help-topic.description: This function adds the back slash (\) character in a string of character to circumvent the ones that cannot be present in a JSON constant (", \, tab, or CR).
help-topic.example: escjson - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: execsql
help-topic.description: This instruction executes a SQL statement against the $$PRODUCT database.
help-topic.example: execsql - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: extended
help-topic.description: Use this keyword in Columns instruction as an option that describes the fact that the list of columns given as a restriction to the Select sentences sent to the database works not only for For, Rewrite, and RewriteByKey instructions, but also for Read and Readlock instructions.
help-topic.example: extended - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: file
help-topic.description: File is used to declare the tables that can be used in a script.
help-topic.example: file - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: fileabre
help-topic.description: fileabre is an internal character string array that gives the list of the abbreviations associated with tables that have been opened by File.
help-topic.example: fileabre - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: filename
help-topic.description: filename is an internal character string array that gives the list of the table that have been opened by File.
help-topic.example: filename - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: first
help-topic.description: First is used as a read or update option to define the first record in the key order.
help-topic.example: first - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: flush
help-topic.description: Flush flushes the write buffer, which is filled by the Writeb instruction.
help-topic.example: flush - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: fmet
help-topic.description: This operator invokes a method on an instance (similar to func). It is used to invoke methods that do return values. For methods that do not return values, use the CallMet instruction.
help-topic.example: fmet - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: fmethod
help-topic.description: This instruction declares a method in a class. It is used to declare methods that return a value. Methods that _do not_ return any value are declared with the Method instruction.
help-topic.example: fmethod - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: freeinstance
help-topic.description: This instruction frees the memory of an instance. The other instances in the same allocation group are not freed; this can be done by using FreeGroup instruction.
help-topic.example: freeinstance - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: freesnapshot
help-topic.description: This built-in method frees the Snapshot of an instance.
help-topic.example: freesnapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: func
help-topic.description: Use this function to Call a subroutine executed with its own local variables class that returns a value. Parameters can be transmitted as values or as references; so that the parameters transmitted by reference can be modified by the subroutine.
help-topic.example: func - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: getaccessorenabled
help-topic.description: This built-in property defines whether the get accessors are enabled on an instance or not.
help-topic.example: getaccessorenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: getbit
help-topic.description: getbit returns the bit value of a bit from an integer. The result is an integer that can have a value of 0 or 1.
help-topic.example: getbit - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: getlogname
help-topic.description: This function returns the file name of the last $$PRODUCT engine log. When the log mode is activated, a file located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable is opened and filled with log information.
help-topic.example: getlogname - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: getmodified
help-topic.description: This built-in method returns the list of properties that have been modified in an instance since the snapshot has been enabled.
help-topic.example: getmodified - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: getseq
help-topic.description: Getseq reads data from a binary file opened by Openi or Openio.
help-topic.example: getseq - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary
help-topic.description: The $$PRODUCT script glossary documents the language elements of the $$PRODUCT scripting engine. Every language element has an associated keyword. A keyword cannot be used as a variable or property name, whenever the case is the same or not. The keyword recognition is not case sensitive, but this will change in the future.
help-topic.example: glossary - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary-datetime
help-topic.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new data type called 'DateTime'. It stores a date and an hour in the same field, while the date data type is limited to the date (usually a business date).
help-topic.example: glossary-datetime - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary-snapshot
help-topic.description: A snapshot is an initial copy of the data stored in a class. It includes all child classes, if there are any. This concept is very useful when managing CRUD operation because it provides the differences between the data read and the modification done.
help-topic.example: glossary-snapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary-structure
help-topic.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced structures. A structure has properties and methods and it is defined in files with an 'STC' extension.
help-topic.example: glossary-structure - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary-updtick
help-topic.description: To manage optimistic update concurrency control, Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new concept called UpdTick. UpdTick, which means update ticks.
help-topic.example: glossary-updtick - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: glossary-uuid
help-topic.description: Version 7 of the $$PRODUCT engine introduced a new data type called UUID Universally Unique Identifier. This is a standard published by the Open Software Foundation (OSF).
help-topic.example: glossary-uuid - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: goto
help-topic.description: Goto performs an unconditional branch from a script to another label.
help-topic.example: goto - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Grizo
help-topic.description: Grizo grays out all or part of the fields in a mask that were previously activated.
help-topic.example: Grizo - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: heapdmp
help-topic.description: This function generates a dump file of memory usage, for debugging purposes.
help-topic.example: heapdmp - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: insa
help-topic.description: Insa is used to insert elements from single-sized arrays, from an index.
help-topic.example: insa - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: iomode
help-topic.description: Use Iomode to assign a value to the different parameters associated with a sequential file opened by Openi, Openio, or Openo when doing an abbreviation.
help-topic.example: iomode - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: isreadonly
help-topic.description: This built-in property returns whether the properties of an instance cannot or can be modified. It can be tested but tot modified.
help-topic.example: isreadonly - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: keylen
help-topic.description: **Caution: keylen has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It is not to be used by developers.**
help-topic.example: keylen - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: keyname
help-topic.description: **Caution: keyname has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It has no more to be used by developers.**
help-topic.example: keyname - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: keyuniq
help-topic.description: **Caution: keyuniq has been deprecated in version $$CURVER. It has no more to be used by developers.**
help-topic.example: keyuniq - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: last
help-topic.description: Use Last as a read or update  option to define the fact that the last record is considered (in the key order).
help-topic.example: last - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: lockwait
help-topic.description: lockwait is an Integer system variable of the Integer type.  It allows you to set the maximum of seconds that each lock attempt will last using the Lock and Readlock instructions. After a first failed lock attempt, the system will retry every second for the duration defined by the lockwait value.
help-topic.example: lockwait - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: logicclose
help-topic.description: This function closes a table logically. It releases the [F] buffer and any filters in a way that makes a further Local File execution much faster than if the table were closed by the Close instruction.
help-topic.example: logicclose - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: maxheap
help-topic.description: The maxheap variable defines the memory (in bytes) that can be allocated in the heap memory of the runtime server process.
help-topic.example: maxheap - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: maxmem
help-topic.description: The maxmem variable defines the memory (in bytes) that can be allocated in the main memory of the runtime server process.
help-topic.example: maxmem - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: maxtab
help-topic.description: This function returns the maximum index used for the first dimension of an array.
help-topic.example: maxtab - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: method
help-topic.description: This instruction declares a method in a class. It is used to declare methods that **_do not_** return any value. Methods that **_do_** return a value are declared with the Fmethod instruction.
help-topic.example: method - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: modified
help-topic.description: This attribute returns whether a property of an instance has been modified since the snapshot has been enabled on the instance.
help-topic.example: modified - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: nbind
help-topic.description: nbind returns the number of indexes in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
help-topic.example: nbind - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: nbzon
help-topic.description: nbzon returns the number of columns in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
help-topic.example: nbzon - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: next-loop
help-topic.description: Use Next in For loops to end the loop.
help-topic.example: next-loop - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: next-read
help-topic.description: Use Next as a read or update option to define the fact that the next record is considered (in the key order).
help-topic.example: next-read - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: nohint
help-topic.description: Use Nohint in the following clause:
help-topic.example: nohint - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Nolign
help-topic.description: nolign is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives the current line value (starting at 1). It is also used in class assignment to identify the current line when a grid in a mask class is assigned from or to a table class.
help-topic.example: Nolign - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Nolign1
help-topic.description: nolign1 is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives the range end on operation involving a range of lines (nolign is the starting point of the range).
help-topic.example: Nolign1 - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: nomap
help-topic.description: Nomap is a character string type variable array that contains the names of the current folder.
help-topic.example: nomap - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: not
help-topic.description: not corresponds to the unary boolean 'not' operator that can also be abbreviated by !.
help-topic.example: not - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: objectnbs
help-topic.description: This built-in property returns the number of properties of an instance.
help-topic.example: objectnbs - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: objecttype
help-topic.description: This built-in property returns the name of the class or representation of an instance.
help-topic.example: objecttype - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: objectvar
help-topic.description: This keyword gives access to the Nth properties of the class associated with a given instance ('N' given as a parameter). It might be important if a pointer to a class is sent to a program that will explore the properties of the class.
help-topic.example: objectvar - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: openlog
help-topic.description: This function sets the $$PRODUCT engine in log mode. When log mode is activated, a file located in the 'TMP' subfolder of the folder defined by the "ADXDIR" environment variable is opened and filled with log information.
help-topic.example: openlog - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: or
help-topic.description: or performs a logical 'or' between two boolean values.
help-topic.example: or - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: order-by
help-topic.description: Order By is a clause that can be used in conjunction with several language keywords:
help-topic.example: order-by - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: prev
help-topic.description: Prev is used as a read or update option to define that the previous record is considered in the key order.
help-topic.example: prev - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: putseq
help-topic.description: Putseq writes data on a binary file opened by Openo or Openio.
help-topic.example: putseq - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: reckey
help-topic.description: reckey is a pseudo key present in database table using this key ensures that the sequential reading order will be optimal (it uses the physical order of the database and thus avoids any data sort when the query is sent to the database). It can be used in For loops.
help-topic.example: reckey - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: resume
help-topic.description: Resume allows you to end the execution of an error handling routine and resume the execution at the line that follows the line where the error was thrown.
help-topic.example: resume - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: reverttosnapshot
help-topic.description: This built-in method reverts an instance to its Snapshot state.
help-topic.example: reverttosnapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: revertupdtick
help-topic.description: This built-in method reverts the updtick value of an instance to its snapshot value.
help-topic.example: revertupdtick - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: rewritebykey
help-topic.description: This instruction updates a record at a given key after checking for edit conflicts. It compares the Updtick value of the [F] record with the updtick value of the corresponding database record, and it updates the database record only if the two ticks are equal.
help-topic.example: rewritebykey - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: sadmem
help-topic.description: The sadmem variable defines the memory (in KB) that is allocated in database access processes as buffers.
help-topic.example: sadmem - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: schar
help-topic.description: This keyword declares a character string with character coded on 1 byte (with a size limited to a value that cannot exceed 255 characters). It is strongly recommended to use Char declaration to avoid issues when dealing with texts that can contains Unicode characters.
help-topic.example: schar - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: seek
help-topic.description: Seek allows you to move the read pointer on a file opened by Openi or Openio. When a null value is sent, it also allows you to flush the write operation performed on a file opened by Openo or Openio.
help-topic.example: seek - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: setaccessorenabled
help-topic.description: This built-in property defines whether the set accessors are enabled on an instance.
help-topic.example: setaccessorenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: setbit
help-topic.description: setbit set a bit of an integer to 0 or 1.  The result is an integer.
help-topic.example: setbit - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: setinstancenosys
help-topic.description: This instruction performs an assignment between the properties of a class instance and the content of a file class or a mask class (for example, an [F:xxxx] table buffer or [M:yyyy] mask class), in one direction or the other. It works exactly as setinstance, but it excludes technical properties from the assignment.
help-topic.example: setinstancenosys - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: snapshot
help-topic.description: This built-in method gives access to the Snapshot image of an instance.
help-topic.example: snapshot - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: snapshotenabled
help-topic.description: This property of a class defines whether the snapshot is enabled on the class (if equal to 1) or disabled (if equal to 0).
help-topic.example: snapshotenabled - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: sql
help-topic.description: Sql allows you to execute a Select statement in the database.
help-topic.example: sql - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: stability
help-topic.description: Stability is a keyword used in a For loop. It is an option dedicated to SQL server that allows the For loop to see only the lines that were in the database when the For loop was started.
help-topic.example: stability - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: stat1
help-topic.description: stat1 is a system variable that contains the number of lines returned by a System instruction.
help-topic.example: stat1 - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: tairec
help-topic.description: tairec returns the size of a record (line) in an opened table. It is an integer variable present in the [G:abv] class associated with a table opened with the _abv_ abbreviation.
help-topic.example: tairec - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: then
help-topic.description: Then is a keyword that can be used between the condition in the If statement and the next statement. It is completely optional: an instruction separator such as the colon or new line is also available.
help-topic.example: then - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: this
help-topic.description: This function gives access to the class instance pointer when executing a method on a class or a property of a class.
help-topic.example: this - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Titcol
help-topic.description: Titcol allows to assign dynamically titles in the columns of a grid present on a screen.
help-topic.example: Titcol - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: tosdata
help-topic.description: This function returns a string value containing an SData _where_ syntax corresponding to a $$PRODUCT valid condition.
help-topic.example: tosdata - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: unescjson
help-topic.description: This function restores a JSON character string to its original state. It removes back slash (\) characters that the  Escjson function would have put in place to enable getting incompatible characters in a JSON constant (", \, tab, or CR).
help-topic.example: unescjson - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: writeb
help-topic.description: Writeb has the same syntax as Write, but allows you to optimize the performances when several insertions of database lines must be done.
help-topic.example: writeb - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: x3script-keywords-glossary
help-topic.description: **The $$PRODUCT scripting language includes a list of keywords. Not all these keywords are used, but they remain reserved words in the language.**
help-topic.example: x3script-keywords-glossary - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: xgetchar
help-topic.description: This function extracts the Nth character of a CHAR or CLOB variable. This function runs much faster than the usual functions such as mid$ or seg$ when managing large CLOB data.
help-topic.example: xgetchar - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: xor
help-topic.description: xor performs an exclusive logical 'or' between two boolean values.
help-topic.example: xor - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Zc
help-topic.description: zc is a system variable used on grid input in masks. It gives access to the current value during controls, initialization, selection and help routines.
help-topic.example: Zc - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Zoncou
help-topic.description: zoncou is a system variable that contains the name of the current field during a control called when an input in a mask is performed.
help-topic.example: Zoncou - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Zonsor
help-topic.description: zonsor is a system variable that contains the name of the last field entered when an input in a mask is performed.
help-topic.example: Zonsor - see category notes and syntax guide.

help-topic.name: Zonsui
help-topic.description: zonsui is a system variable that can be assigned during a control called during an input in a mask in order to change the order of input by reassigning the next field to be inputted.
help-topic.example: Zonsui - see category notes and syntax guide.

ui-command.name: Additm
ui-command.description: Observed UI composition command that adds an item dynamically.
ui-command.signature: Additm POSITION Titled TEXT To TARGET
invariants: Keep the target explicit.

ui-command.name: Addmen
ui-command.description: Observed command that adds a menu entry dynamically.
invariants: Use stable identifiers.
Keep the action traceable.

ui-command.name: Boxact
ui-command.description: Observed box activation command used in interactive screen flows.
invariants: Apply it to an explicit box identifier.

ui-command.name: Boxinp
ui-command.description: Observed boxed-input command that captures a response through a UI box.
invariants: Keep the target response variable explicit.

ui-command.name: Discombo
ui-command.description: Observed UI command that disables one or more combo choices.
invariants: Make the disabled values explicit.

ui-command.name: Dislbox
ui-command.description: Observed command that disables or closes a list-box interaction path.
invariants: Use it as part of explicit UI state transitions.

ui-command.name: Fillbox
ui-command.description: Observed command that populates a list or box widget from a source or keyed selection.
invariants: Use explicit source criteria for reproducible behavior.

ui-command.name: Hlpbox
ui-command.description: Observed helper command that opens or resolves contextual help for a symbol or screen object.
invariants: Keep the help target deterministic.

ui-command.name: Onevent
ui-command.description: Observed UI event-binding construct that routes screen events to handlers.
invariants: Keep the event name explicit.
Route to a stable label or routine.
ui-command.example: Onevent CONTROL Call MY_CONTROL From MYSCRIPT

ui-command.name: Report
ui-command.description: Observed reporting command used to launch a report with parameters and capture a return value.
invariants: Keep the target and parameter set explicit.

ui-command.name: Saizo
ui-command.description: Observed screen-layout command that resizes or adjusts a mask area or field zone.
invariants: Use it carefully because it changes layout behavior.

ui-command.name: Setlbox
ui-command.description: Observed command that sets the active list box or list-box selection context.
invariants: Keep the target list box explicit.

ui-command.name: Setmok
ui-command.description: Observed mask-state command used to change editability or interaction state.
invariants: Use explicit mode values.
Apply it to the intended mask scope.

ui-command.name: Transmask
ui-command.description: Observed command that transfers values between mask contexts.
invariants: Keep source and target masks explicit.

runtime-variable.name: adxpam
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: adxpid
runtime-variable.description: adxpid returns the process **ID** that executes the $$PRODUCT code on the $$PRODUCT server.

runtime-variable.name: adxtcp
runtime-variable.description: adxtcp returns the IP service number used to connect on the $$PRODUCT process for the server. It returns a numerical value.

runtime-variable.name: adxtrans
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: adxtyp
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: bookabr
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: cop$
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: date$
runtime-variable.description: This function returns the current date as given from the system in a Date format. This date time is obtained from the process server and is the local time (the timezone of the server).

runtime-variable.name: datetime$
runtime-variable.description: This function returns the current datetime in a DateTime variable. This date time is given by default in the Greenwitch time zone and got from the process server.

runtime-variable.name: dir$
runtime-variable.description: dir$ returns the path of the directory where the $$PRODUCT runtime engine is installed.

runtime-variable.name: errl
runtime-variable.description: errl returns the number of the line in a script where an error occurred. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

runtime-variable.name: errm
runtime-variable.description: errm returns an additional error message detail when an error occurs. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

runtime-variable.name: errn
runtime-variable.description: errn returns a numeric error code when an error occurs. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

runtime-variable.name: errnremote
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: errp
runtime-variable.description: errp returns the name of the script where an error occurred. It can be used in the error handling routine set by Onerrgo.

runtime-variable.name: freeheap
runtime-variable.description: The 'Freeheap' function returns the number of bytes not used in the heap memory of the runtime server process.

runtime-variable.name: freemem
runtime-variable.description: The 'Freemem' function returns the number of bytes not used in the main memory of the runtime server process.

runtime-variable.name: fstat
runtime-variable.description: fstat is a numeric status that is returned upon execution of a database operation, a sequential file operation, or a lock instruction.

runtime-variable.name: GERR
runtime-variable.description: Observed global error flag used to signal that the current operation failed.
invariants: Pair it with an explanatory message when possible.

runtime-variable.name: getuuid
runtime-variable.description: getUuid generates a new UUID.

runtime-variable.name: GMESSAGE
runtime-variable.description: Observed global message variable used to surface user-facing error or status text.
invariants: Set it alongside the matching status flag or error path.

runtime-variable.name: GOK
runtime-variable.description: Observed global success flag used to continue or abort the current workflow.
invariants: Change it intentionally because downstream flow often depends on it.

runtime-variable.name: GPOINT
runtime-variable.description: Observed global entry-point variable used to select a framework hook before calling shared execution logic.
invariants: Use stable hook names because execution dispatch depends on them.

runtime-variable.name: indcum
runtime-variable.description: indcum is a short integer variable used as a default index by the sigma function. It is only relevant when a single sigma loop is used; otherwise, you need to name the variable used as a sigma index.

runtime-variable.name: inpmode
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: maskcou
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: nbruser
runtime-variable.description: Runtime variable exposed by the 4GL execution environment.

runtime-variable.name: null
runtime-variable.description: This keyword represents the **_null_** instance.

runtime-variable.name: nulluuid
runtime-variable.description: This keyword represents the **_null_** UUID constant.

runtime-variable.name: pi
runtime-variable.description: pi returns an approximated value of the 𝜋 (pi) number.

runtime-variable.name: status
runtime-variable.description: status is a system variable that contains the return status of an entry statement.

runtime-variable.name: time
runtime-variable.description: time returns the local time of the current process server. The value returned is the number of seconds elapsed since midnight.

runtime-variable.name: time$
runtime-variable.description: This function returns the current time, as given from the system, as a string using the hh:mm:ss format. This time is obtained from the process server and is the local time (in the timezone of the server).

runtime-variable.name: timestamp$
runtime-variable.description: timestamp$ returns a string value containing a time stamp, which is the number of milliseconds elapsed since the first of January 1970, at 00:00:00 time GMT.

runtime-variable.name: trtcou
runtime-variable.description: trtcou returns the name of the current script.

runtime-variable.name: uuid$
runtime-variable.description: uuid$ generates a new canconical (string) UUID.

The only solution is to check the user Readlock.

type.name: Clbfile

type.name: date
type.description: This keyword declares a date variable where the value is within the range from [1/1/1600] to [31/12/9999], plus a null date [0/0/0].

type.name: datetime
type.description: This keyword declares a DateTime variable.

type.name: Decimal
type.description: This keyword declares a binary coded decimal value with up to 32 digits of precision.

type.name: Double
type.description: This keyword declares a double precision floating value with a precision that may depend on the hardware. It is therefore **strongly recommended** not to use this keyword anymore, and to replace it by Decimal variables.

type.name: Float
type.description: This keyword declares a floating value with a precision that may depend on the hardware. It is therefore **strongly recommended** not to use this keyword anymore, and to replace it with Decimal variables.

type.name: Instance
type.description: This keyword declares an instance of a class or a representation. The instance is Null initially. You must allocate it with NewInstance before reading or writing its properties.

type.name: Integer
type.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from -2^31 to 2^31-1.

type.name: Libelle
type.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from 0 to 255. This declaration is deprecated and should be replaced by Tinyint.

type.name: Shortint
type.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from -32768 to 32767.

type.name: Tinyint
type.description: This keyword declares an integer variable where the value is within the range from 0 to 255.

type.name: Uuident
type.description: This keyword declares a UUID variable.

data-access.name: Link
data-access.description: Creates a joined alias for iterating across related tables with explicit keys.
invariants: Name the joined alias explicitly.
Prefer readable join keys over implicit coupling.
notes: Common in selection and list-style routines.

data-access.name: Local File
data-access.description: Declares a local table handle for the current routine, often guarded by clalev checks in reusable code.
invariants: Avoid reopening already-open aliases.
Keep abbreviations stable inside the routine.
data-access.example: If clalev([F:BPC])=0 : Local File BPCUSTOMER [BPC] : Endif
notes: Observed as a standard safety idiom in project code.

ui-runtime-state.name: mkstat
ui-runtime-state.description: Observed runtime mask status variable assigned during validation or interaction flow.
invariants: Set it deliberately because it changes UI behavior.

ui-runtime-state.name: pcolor
ui-runtime-state.description: Observed runtime color state variable used in UI rendering or highlighting.
invariants: Treat it as presentation state, not domain data.

error-handling.name: Onerrgo
error-handling.description: Declares an error label and redirects runtime errors to an explicit recovery or resume path.
invariants: Keep the error label close to the routine.
Resume only when the failure path is understood.
notes: Useful for legacy-style guarded sections, but should remain explicit.

transaction.name: Trbegin
transaction.description: Starts an explicit transaction block that should end with Commit or Rollback after checking fstat.
invariants: Always pair with explicit error handling.
Do not leave transaction outcome implicit.
transaction.example: Trbegin [BPC] / Rewrite [BPC] / If fstat : Rollback : Else : Commit : Endif
notes: Promoted by snippets and aligned with explicitness requirements.
